name: JWT encode/decode (HS256)
task: Create /tmp/eval_jwt/jwt.py with two functions: jwt_encode(payload: dict, secret: str, algorithm='HS256') returns a JWT string (header.payload.signature — three base64url-encoded segments joined by dots, no '=' padding; the signature is HMAC-SHA256 over the ASCII bytes of 'header.payload'); jwt_decode(token: str, secret: str, now=None) returns the payload dict (raises ValueError on bad signature, malformed token, or expired 'exp' claim). The 'exp' claim is optional — a payload without it never expires; when present it is a numeric UNIX timestamp and the token is expired iff now >= exp. Compare signatures with hmac.compare_digest. If now is None, use time.time().
